pwa.installText: Add fitlo to your home screen for faster access.
pwa.iosHint: In Safari, tap the Share button and choose “Add to Home Screen”.

footer.tagline: The bilingual coaching & training-program platform — for coaches and their athletes.

about.title: About fitlo
about.intro: fitlo is a tool for fitness coaches who want to write professional training programs and support their athletes — without scattered files and lost DMs.
about.mission: Most coaches lose hours to repetitive work: rebuilding the same programs, sending files over chat, and chasing client details. fitlo brings it into one place so your attention stays on what actually matters — training quality and client progress.
about.value1: Persian and RTL from the ground up — not a half-translated foreign tool.
about.value2: Write a program against a phone number; the student doesn't need an account yet.
about.value3: Your own exercise library with GIFs and video links, plus a clean PDF export.
about.value4: A public page for every coach, collecting student requests directly.

forms.hasErrors: Please fill in the required fields

landing.title: Write and track training programs, together
landing.subtitle: Coaches write day-by-day programs and send them straight to their students; students see their program on their phone the moment it's ready. Start free, with just a phone number.
landing.ctaHint: Sign in or sign up free — all you need is your phone number.

features.library: Your own exercise library with GIFs

landing.howSubtitle: Whether you coach or train, get going in three simple steps.

coachSteps.heading: For coaches
coachSteps.s1Text: Add your exercises once, then write day-by-day programs with supersets and export a clean PDF.
coachSteps.s2Text: The student doesn't need an account yet — programs link automatically when they sign up with that number.
coachSteps.s3Text: Post your link-in-bio and collect student requests straight in your inbox.

studentSteps.heading: For students
studentSteps.s1Text: Open the coach's public page, fill in your details, and send a program request.
studentSteps.s2Text: Track your request status in your panel; if it's declined, you'll see the reason.
studentSteps.s3Text: Programs appear automatically once published, and you can download them as a PDF.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Do I need to install anything?
faq.a1: No. fitlo is a web app that runs in your browser — you can also add it to your phone's home screen.
faq.a2: With your phone number. The first time you verify by SMS code and choose a password; after that you sign in with just that password (or a one-time code whenever you prefer).
faq.a3: Coaches get a 15-day free trial, then can subscribe for 3, 6, or 12 months. It's free for students.
faq.q4: What if a coach wrote a program before I signed up?
faq.a4: Just register with the same number the coach entered — every program links to your account automatically.

pwaSection.title: The fitlo app
pwaSection.subtitle: iPhone, Android, or desktop — install fitlo on your device right now and open it faster next time.
pwaSection.alreadyInstalled: fitlo is installed on this device
pwaSection.manualHint: Open your browser menu and choose "Add to Home Screen" or "Install app".

onboarding.title: Getting started
onboarding.step1Text: Add a photo and bio so you look professional to students.
onboarding.step2Text: Build your exercise library so you can write programs fast.
onboarding.step3Text: Create and publish a day-by-day program for a student.
onboarding.step4Text: Add your card number and price so students see them on the request form.

studentHome.help1: Programs your coaches write for you appear here automatically.
studentHome.help2: To get a new program, open a coach's public link and send a request.
studentHome.help3: Track your request status under "My requests".

billing.title: Subscription
billing.subtitle: Keep building and editing programs with an active subscription.
billing.startTrialDesc: 15 days free to build and edit programs — no card required. This trial can only be activated once.
billing.simulateNote: Test mode: no real gateway configured — payment is simulated.
billing.bannerExpired: Your trial has ended. Subscribe to create or edit programs (your existing data stays viewable).
billing.bannerNoTrial: You haven't activated your free trial yet. Activate 15 free days to start building programs.

profile.title: Coach profile
profile.subtitle: This information may be shown to your students.
profile.name: Display name
profile.tagsPlaceholder: Type a tag and press Enter
profile.publicLinkHint: Share this link anywhere — anyone can request a program from you.
profile.handleHint: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens (3–30 chars).

publicPage.title: Your public page
publicPage.what: This is the page students see — your name, photo, bio and contact links — and where they send you a program request.
publicPage.noHandle: You don't have a handle yet, so your public page isn't live. Pick one and save your profile.
publicPage.unsaved: Your new handle isn't saved yet — until you save, the link above still points to the old one.
publicPage.tip: Put the link in your Instagram bio, story highlights and pinned post so requests land straight in your inbox.

exercises.title: Exercise library
exercises.subtitle: Build your reusable exercise catalog.
exercises.empty: No exercises yet — create your first one.
exercises.name: Name
exercises.invalidFile: Please choose a GIF, PNG, JPG, or WebP image
exercises.videoUrlPlaceholder: YouTube, Aparat, or any video URL
exercises.help1: Add each exercise once, then reuse it across every program you write.
exercises.help2: Categories (e.g. Chest, Legs, Back) keep your exercises tidy so you can find them fast — create them from <action>Manage categories</action>.
exercises.help3: Attach a GIF and a video link to any exercise so your students can see the correct form.
exercises.categoryHint: Optional — used to organize and quickly filter your exercises.
exercises.categoryHintEmpty: You haven't created any categories yet. It's optional — add them from "Manage categories" on the exercises page.

categories.title: Categories
categories.subtitle: Group your exercises (e.g. Chest, Legs).
categories.exists: A category with this name already exists
categories.deleteConfirm: Delete this category? Exercises stay but become uncategorized.
categories.what: Categories exist purely to organize your own exercises (e.g. Chest, Legs). You use them to filter quickly while writing a program — students never see them.
